mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into higher short-term equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during monetary tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation readings coincide with positive forward returns in > 50% of observations over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth falls below 1.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.02 across all rolling 2-year windows despite positive GDP prints.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and inventory drawdowns, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative production shocks are followed by positive forward returns in > 55% of cases over a 3-year window.
